title: Aquatic Park: An insider's guide to the historic SF park
content: # Your guide to Aquatic Park, a San Francisco beach park frozen in time. A member of the Dolphin Club swims at Aquatic Park in San Francisco, Calif., on Wednesday, May 20, 2014. Aquatic Park is a historic beach park located on San Francisco’s northeast waterfront. It’s surrounded by Hyde Street Pier, Fisherman’s Wharf, the Maritime Museum and the iconic Ghirardelli Square, with views of the Golden Gate Bridge and Marin just beyond its waters. While the park is in need of investment, there’s still plenty to enjoy about this historic, accessible beach and beloved swimming spot, located in the heart of many of San Francisco’s must-see tourist attractions. ## Swimming at Aquatic Park Cove. At the center of this committed culture is Aquatic Park Cove, where the Dolphin Club and South End Rowing Club, neighboring historic swim clubs, together boast more than 3,000 registered members, many of whom participate in organized swims and races year-round here. Aquatic Park visitors walk along the promenade in front of the Maritime Museum in San Francisco, Calif.

title: SAN FRANCISCO MARITIME NATIONAL HISTORICAL ...
